SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.logical_expr

Interface AggregateUDF

public interface AggregateUDF
A user-defined aggregate function that can be registered with a SessionContext.

Implement this interface to create custom aggregate functions usable in SQL queries. Unlike scalar UDFs (which process row-by-row), aggregate UDFs accumulate state across multiple rows to produce a single result per group.

Example usage: 

AggregateUDF mySum = new AggregateUDF() {
    public String name() { return "my_sum"; }
    public Signature signature() { return new Signature(Volatility.IMMUTABLE); }
    public Field returnField(List<Field> argFields) {
        return Field.nullable("my_sum", new ArrowType.Int(64, true));
    }
    public Accumulator createAccumulator() { return new MySumAccumulator(); }
};
ctx.registerUdaf(mySum, allocator);
DataFrame result = ctx.sql("SELECT my_sum(value) FROM t");
See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: AggregateUDF

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field>

    Returns the name of this aggregate function as it will appear in SQL.

  • Method Details

    • name

      String name()
      Returns the name of this aggregate function as it will appear in SQL.

      Example: 

      @Override
public String name() {
    return "my_sum";
}
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: AggregateUDF::name

    • signature

      Signature signature()
      Returns the signature of this aggregate function, including its volatility.

      Example: 

      @Override
public Signature signature() {
    return new Signature(Volatility.IMMUTABLE);
}
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: AggregateUDF::signature

    • returnField

      org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field returnField(List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field> argFields)
      Determines the return field (name + type) given the argument fields.

      Example: 

      @Override
public Field returnField(List<Field> argFields) {
    return Field.nullable("my_sum", new ArrowType.Int(64, true));
}
      Parameters:
      argFields - the fields of the input arguments
      Returns:
      the field describing the return type
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: AggregateUDF::return_field

    • createAccumulator

      Accumulator createAccumulator()
      Creates a new accumulator instance for aggregating values.

      DataFusion calls this method to create one accumulator per group. The accumulator tracks state across multiple calls to Accumulator.updateBatch(java.util.List<org.apache.arrow.vector.FieldVector>) and produces a final result via Accumulator.evaluate().

      Example: 

      @Override
public Accumulator createAccumulator() {
    return new MySumAccumulator();
}
      Returns:
      a new accumulator instance
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: AggregateUDFImpl::accumulator

    • stateFields

      default List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field> stateFields(org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field returnField)
      Returns the fields used to store intermediate aggregation state.

      Override this method when your accumulator tracks multiple state values (e.g., AVG tracks both sum and count). The default returns a single field with the return type.

      Example: 

      @Override
public List<Field> stateFields(Field returnField) {
    return List.of(
        Field.nullable("sum", new ArrowType.FloatingPoint(FloatingPointPrecision.DOUBLE)),
        Field.nullable("count", new ArrowType.Int(64, true))
    );
}
      Parameters:
      returnField - the return field of the aggregate function
      Returns:
      the fields describing the intermediate state
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: AggregateUDFImpl::state_fields

    • coerceTypes

      default List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field> coerceTypes(List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field> argFields)
      Performs type coercion on the input argument fields. The default implementation returns the argument fields unchanged.

      Example: 

      @Override
public List<Field> coerceTypes(List<Field> argFields) {
    return List.of(Field.nullable("arg0", new ArrowType.Int(64, true)));
}
      Parameters:
      argFields - the fields of the input arguments
      Returns:
      the coerced fields
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: AggregateUDFImpl::coerce_types
